comment: Fleet registry for the cold-start pinger (.github/workflows/coldstart-pinger.yml). Per run, each app gets a concurrent burst of `burst` requests to its `page` path and `burst` to its `api` path — one batch captures both the cold start (the request that boots an instance) and warm behavior (the rest). Apps with `health: true` run nextrs >= 0.3.7, which stamps x-nextrs-cold/-boot-id/-uptime-ms on EVERY response, so page and api samples classify identically. Apps with `health: false` predate the headers; timing-only until they upgrade.

apps.name: react-todos
apps.note: flip to health:true when nextrs 0.3.7 is published and the app redeployed

apps.name: onenote-extractor
apps.note: vendored 0.3.3; page 303s to auth — timing only

apps.name: hhh-next
apps.note: nextrs 0.2.1; /api/health was mirrored for the June bench rounds
